name: D+Q 20-week adverse-event aggregate result
arm_details: ClinicalTrials.gov adverseEventsModule eventGroups report D+Q and untreated-control aggregate counts for other adverse events, serious adverse events, and deaths from baseline to approximately 20 weeks. The comparative effect below applies only to the registry other adverse-event aggregate, where both compared arms have explicit participant counts.

group_values.group_label: D+Q participants with other adverse events
Untreated-control participants with other adverse events
D+Q participants with serious adverse events
Untreated-control participants with serious adverse events

effect.raw_text: ClinicalTrials.gov adverseEventsModule eventGroups report otherNumAffected/otherNumAtRisk of 23/30 for D+Q and 5/30 for untreated control; risk ratio and approximate 95% CI were calculated from those arm counts using the log risk-ratio standard error.

analysis.method: Risk ratio calculated from registry other adverse-event aggregate counts (23/30 divided by 5/30); standard error is on the log risk-ratio scale.
analysis.effect_note: The comparative effect applies only to participants affected by other adverse events in the ClinicalTrials.gov eventGroups aggregate. Serious adverse events and deaths are retained descriptively as explicit 0/30 counts in both compared arms.

event_specific_counts.arm_label: D+Q participants with other adverse events
event_specific_counts.interpretation: The registry adverseEventsModule reports 23 D+Q participants affected by other adverse events among 30 at risk.

event_specific_counts.arm_label: Untreated-control participants with other adverse events
event_specific_counts.interpretation: The registry adverseEventsModule reports 5 untreated-control participants affected by other adverse events among 30 at risk.

zero_handling.rationale: Both compared arms have explicit nonzero other-adverse-event participant counts, so no blank-cell or zero-imputation rule is needed for this registry aggregate comparison.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract serious adverse event sentence
provenance.note: Abstract reports no serious adverse events were observed.

statement: ClinicalTrials.gov registry extraction shows other adverse events were more common in the D+Q arm than untreated control (23/30 versus 5/30; calculated risk ratio 4.60), while serious adverse events and deaths were 0/30 in both compared arms.
caveats: The adverse_event preferred_term field preserves a ClinicalTrials.gov aggregate category, not an individual MedDRA preferred term.
The calculated risk ratio and uncertainty apply only to the registry other-adverse-event aggregate, not to serious adverse events, deaths, or individual preferred-term rows.
This aggregate record does not enumerate every adverse-event preferred term; term-specific extraction should remain separate for preferred-term safety synthesis.
Safety interpretation should account for the open-label design and the source distinction between registry aggregate counts and publication table preferred-term rows.
